areas.title: Data-Centric AI
areas.short: Advancing AI by improving the data it learns from — through better curation, quality assessment, diversity analysis, and rigorous benchmarking for clinical reliability.
areas.description: Advancing AI by improving the data it learns from — through better curation, quality assessment, diversity analysis, and rigorous benchmarking for clinical reliability.

areas.title: Intelligent Clinical Tools
areas.short: Designing AI-powered tools that integrate meaningfully into clinical workflows — reducing burden, supporting decision-making, and adapting to real-world healthcare settings.
areas.description: Designing AI-powered tools that integrate meaningfully into clinical workflows — reducing burden, supporting decision-making, and adapting to real-world healthcare settings.

areas.title: Simulation & Virtual Trials
areas.short: Leveraging physics-based simulation and virtual patient models to evaluate AI rigorously, safely, and at scale — where real-world clinical testing alone is limited or costly.
areas.description: Leveraging physics-based simulation and virtual patient models to evaluate AI rigorously, safely, and at scale — where real-world clinical testing alone is limited or costly.

areas.title: Synthetic & Generative Data
areas.short: Creating controllable, privacy-preserving synthetic datasets and generative models for AI training, evaluation, and reproducible experimentation in medicine.
areas.description: Creating controllable, privacy-preserving synthetic datasets and generative models for AI training, evaluation, and reproducible experimentation in medicine.

areas.title: Trustworthy & Clinically Grounded AI
areas.short: Developing evaluation frameworks that go beyond standard metrics — testing AI under clinically meaningful conditions to build systems worthy of real-world trust.
areas.description: Developing evaluation frameworks that go beyond standard metrics — testing AI under clinically meaningful conditions to build systems worthy of real-world trust.

featured.title: Virtual Lung Screening Trial (VLST)
featured.summary: A large-scale virtual imaging trial simulating low-dose CT lung cancer screening across diverse patient populations, scanner models, and acquisition protocols — enabling rigorous, controlled evaluation of AI screening tools without the cost and limitations of traditional clinical trials.
featured.impact: Enables scalable, population-level evaluation of lung cancer screening AI under conditions that are impossible to replicate in real-world trials alone.

featured.title: SYN-LUNGS
featured.summary: A comprehensive synthetic data generation pipeline that produces anatomically realistic, pathology-conditioned lung CT images with full ground-truth annotations — supporting AI development, evaluation, and reproducibility.
featured.impact: Provides the research community with open, controllable, and fully annotated imaging data to accelerate development and fair benchmarking of thoracic AI models.

featured.title: NodMAISI
featured.summary: An intelligent tool for automated lung nodule measurement, analysis, and reporting in CT imaging — designed to reduce inter-reader variability and support standardized clinical workflows in pulmonary nodule management.
featured.impact: Brings consistency and efficiency to a critical step in lung cancer detection, directly addressing a real clinical bottleneck in radiology practice.

gallery.title: CPU-Index Framework
gallery.description: A data-centric framework for measuring and improving the coverage, precision, and utility of medical imaging datasets for AI development.

gallery.title: DukeSeg
gallery.description: Segmentation tools for anatomical structures in medical imaging, built for integration into clinical and research pipelines.

gallery.title: Foundation Models for CT
gallery.description: Exploring large-scale pre-trained models adapted for CT imaging tasks — from organ segmentation to anomaly detection.

gallery.title: Digital Human Phantoms
gallery.description: Computationally generated patient models with realistic anatomy and pathology for physics-based imaging simulation.

gallery.title: Pathology-Conditioned CT Generation
gallery.description: Generative models that produce CT images conditioned on specific pathologies, enabling controlled experiments and data augmentation.

gallery.title: Privacy-Preserving Data Augmentation
gallery.description: Synthetic data methods designed to expand training datasets while preserving patient privacy and maintaining clinical fidelity.

gallery.title: Open Synthetic Datasets
gallery.description: Publicly available synthetic imaging datasets with full annotations, designed to support reproducible AI research and benchmarking.

gallery.title: Robustness & Stress Testing for AI
gallery.description: Systematic evaluation of AI model behavior under distribution shifts, edge cases, and clinically challenging scenarios.
